item.title: Dumping memes at a 45° angle
item.description:
So much space for forehead kisses...
Sorry to anyone who dared subscribe to me. I'm about to dump out as much as I can in an hour to clear my phone. These go back to last October obviously.
Miss this king
Oh shit, it me
We did it, friends! We made AI dumber!
I mentioned this to my dad and he's just like "well yeah, the system..." uh, dad, we uh... nevermind
Never let John do what Jodie would have gotten right
As one does
Same. This year so far, I had a mess at work, got the worst food poisoning of my life that lasted 5 days while out of town, lost my mom, and developed neck problems. But I got a dog!
I have recipes
True, I grew up watching Captain Picard from my dad's lap
We need to recreate this as a festival
Sick burn, Glinda
Why is this so accurate
Saved this for my gen psych class to remind them not to self diagnose. Let someone who has no idea what is going on in your head do it...
I mean...
Sent this to my sister and she heartily agreed
I hated artificial grape and cherry flavors my whole life, but I love fake banana. Gimme some of that extinct banana species flavor baby.
Shout-out to my old college GBF!!!
It really doesn't
Ope, that'll be a tough one
My dad has that one!!
I opened mine last year in a public restaurant with a friend. We laughed so hard.
Glory to you and your problem solving abilities
Pet tax! Violet (dog) really wants to snuggle, but Clarice (cat) would rather not.
item.dc:creator: AnxiousCuttlefish

item.title: I stole this dump, so you don't have to. Tuesday random meme dump of pure chaos
item.description:
1. The age of men is over, the time of chicken has come!
2. He's been hurt too many times, got it right this time
3. Movie extra records a kid practicing lines
4. A truly unhackable door
5. The wingman we all need
6
7
8. You can take only one on a date - left or right?
9
10. Alien Animal Planet: "Here we have a female of homo species doing a traditional mating dance. Let's see if it works!"
11. Dog helps settle a dispute
12
13. Cleaning up the streets
14. No need for a paternity test over here
15. Thank you for making it to the end of the dump. I hope you don't mind me sharing that for the past year and a half, I’ve been working on a game that’s very close to my heart. "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)" is a quirky adventure crafted with love and passion, with my brother by my side, helping every step of the way. As indie developers, your support means the world to us. By adding our game to your wishlist, you’ll help us reach more players and make a big impact in the gaming community. Here it is: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ We can’t do this without you. Thank you for supporting our dream and joining us on this wild adventure!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: I'm so happy for them. Shelter dogs are the best dogs.
item.dc:creator: Rammsteinstochter

item.title: More memes? Yes! 46th edition
item.description:
Third in an hour. I have enough for like 12 more full sized dumps. I should save more fiber in my phone.
My dad made me sad pointing out that their feathers are clipped. Hopefully it's in a rescue and recovering well.
My niece knows the German phrase, "you have a very punchable face". I need to memorize it.
My mom and I used to watch old cheesy horror movies together. I haven't watched one since she died. Not the same without her pointing out strings holding up the props.
Nostalgic a/f, 5 stars
So called out rn
I'm reading the witcher books. Dandelion is the greatest. Love that dumbass.
If you put ketchup on noodles we can't be friends
This has more weight in December
I've been to both places on wildlife tours. This is super accurate.
She licked my Lighthouse until I shone out to sea
I actually sent this to my boss because I thought she might be able to do something about the dip wagon who runs our grounds team...
My mom had printed weekly calendars for all of us in front of the back door when I was a teenager. She still would ask if I worked Tuesday. Yes. I work every Tuesday, 4-9pm.
Fair
Saved for mom before she died. She used a wheelchair for 13 years. It's disgusting how inaccessible the US is.
That's cold...... [gonzo face]
Okay that hurts more than assuming I did it on purpose
Just lol'ed at my own memes because they've been in here so long I forgot how funny they are
The worms are for my niece. She loves them so much she has 10 rainbow worms tied to the ceiling fan in her bedroom.
I think there's a young Paul and an old Paul and that's where the extra is
Dog tax: Maggie (left) also not wanting to snuggle with Violet
item.dc:creator: AnxiousCuttlefish

item.title: Images of Note: i hope it works
item.description:
I think we need to stress this about people who babble on about "the Market" like it's a God, it's literally rich cunts.
Here's another load of facts that will break your conception of history: * The earliest person photographed was born in 1753 at the latest, when they were born the Holy Roman Empire still existed. * The last human Sikh guru died a year after Scotland lost it's independence. * JRR Tolkien had beef with the Beatles and encouraged Led Zeppelin to write songs about his works. * The Duke of Wellington lived long enough to have his photo taken. * The (generally accepted) inventor of the first practical fuel-burning engine was born four years after the Stuart restoration.
this is true
I once had someone take out their card while it said "don't remove your card" and it broke the machine and everyone acted like it was my fault.
creechur
hey, maybe we should talk more about how a country is threatening a prosecutor and their family over warrants for war crimes.
b o l a s
a himbo and a herbo find love :)
Oh shit
Kinda wild that while countries in the developing world are outlawing child marriage the US not only still has it but have people openly advocating for it to remain legal.
Ok now I ship Penny and Leah.
The balls on the man
krobus my beloved
nyuum
basically what I was doing before this
fuck me that's a real pirate!
That...is the last thing I expected.
Hey transport for Edinburgh: how can we get you to replace the logo on the front with a little face?
http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream (m/w/f around 1830 UK time) http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging

item.title: It was a good run Steve
item.dc:creator: SocialKeked

item.title: This is Estelle and Arthur. What they had to go through is crazy
item.description:
Estelle Peck was one of the only non-Japanese people in the internment camps during the 1940s. Why? Because her husband, Arthur Ishigo, was Japanese. After President Roosevelt signed Executive Order 9066, Arthur was ordered to report to the temporary detention center at the Pomona Fairgrounds. Estelle faced a decision – stay with her husband of 13 years and be incarcerated or remain in Los Angeles alone...
The two of them, both artists, met at an art institute. In order to avoid American anti-miscegenation laws, they drove to Tijuana, Mexico in 1928 to get married. Being an interracial couple, they faced hardship and Estelle was disowned by her family. The couple lived in the Japanese American community of Los Angeles, and were avid campers – finding refuge from racial prejudice in nature. That refuge didn't last long though. Following the attack on Pearl Harbor, the couple faced heightened discrimination. Arthur and all other ethnic Japanese who worked at Paramount Studios were fired on December 8, 1941, one day after the attack. A few weeks later, Estelle was fired from her job as an art teacher at the Hollywood Art Center due to her Japanese surname.
Estelle was informed that if she chose to go with her husband to the camps, she would not have any privileges due to her race and would have the same status as the Japanese American prisoners. She joined her husband anyway. They were incarcerated at the Heart Mountain Relocation Center in Wyoming. During their time at Heart Mountain, Estelle continued to use her artwork to document their lives.
She sketched and painted and felt accepted into the Japanese American community. Ishigo later wrote, "Strange as it may sound, in this desperate and lonely place, I felt accepted for the first time in my life. The government had declared me a Japanese and I no longer saw myself as white. I was a Japanese American. My fellow Heart Mountain residents took me in as one of their own." She chose charcoal sketches and pencil drawings as her main media because she found watercolors to be "too clean and untroubled" to capture the experiences of camp.
After the war, with no work and no place to live, Estelle and Arthur lived in segregated trailer camps outside of Los Angeles. Arthur took odd jobs at fish canneries, but was deeply depressed from the experience of incarceration. The couple lived in poverty for years following the end of the war. On August 19, 1957, Arthur Ishigo died of cancer at the age of 55. Following Arthur's death, Estelle took a job as a mimeograph operator to earn money.
In 1983, documentary filmmakers and former Heart Mountain prisoners tracked down Estelle living in a basement apartment in Los Angeles. She was the subject of the documentary short film "Days of Waiting: The Life & Art of Estelle Ishigo." The short won a Peabody Award and an Academy Award for Best Documentary (Short Subject). You can it for free here: https://archive.org/details/Days_Of_Waiting_The_Life_and_Art_of_Esteele_Ishigo_1990 We're currently living in troubled times. Fascists are feeling emboldened. As Mark Twain is reputed to have once said, "“History doesn't repeat itself but it often rhymes”

item.title: Created these as gifts for my Music Students. A Composer teaspoon set.
item.description: Using a fiber laser. Each spoon has music from the respective composer on the scoop! Here's the 'Liszt' of composers, and their featured music. *Beethoven with Moonlight Sonata *Mozart with Rondo Alla Turca *Liszt with La Campanella *Chopin with Nocturne in Eb I had a few requests to make extra sets, so I've put them on Etsy if anyone's interested. https://artisanestate.etsy.com/listing/1343652129

item.dc:creator: sirbinlid

item.title: Snow days suck as an adult
item.description: I live in the largest city in Alaska. The past couple of winters the city had record snowfall that resulted in the entire city shutting down for multiple days at a time because it was physically impossible for most vehicles to drive anywhere. This was due in part to the mayor and governor both selling off most of the snow removal vehicles to pay for their own pet projects. The mayor then also fired most of the workers who knew how to operate the remaining snow plows and put his unqualified buddies in charge of snow removal. So when the plows DID actually come thru they did a piss poor job of plowing the streets and then left them in that state for weeks at a time. The main roads would eventually become drivable, but most of the side streets (i.e.: neighborhoods) remained so badly plowed that many of them were reduced to a single lane. After 2 years of this in a row the mayor was finally kicked out of office this year (partly because he also kept doing various illegal crap like stealing funds from other projects to try to fund his own project).
